---
name: Agent-spec-ready issue
about: A problem or gap written so an agent can run spec-writer on it directly, without re-investigating.
title: ""
labels: []
---

<!--
Fill in every section with concrete, verifiable content — no placeholders
left in. This issue is the raw material for spec-writer's interview: the
better the evidence and boundaries here, the fewer round-trips the
interview needs. Delete this comment block once done.
-->

## Problem

<1 paragraph: what's actually wrong or missing, observed behavior vs
expected — no implementation detail, no proposed fix yet.>

## Evidence / Reproduction

<The proof this is real, not a guess: exact commands run, files/lines
involved, commit hashes, actual output/error text. If this has happened
more than once, cite each occurrence. This is what turns "I think X is
broken" into a requirement spec-writer can turn into a GIVEN/WHEN/THEN.>

## Impact

<Why this matters and to whom — what breaks, what it costs, what silently
goes wrong if left as-is. This becomes the spec's Purpose.>

## Suggested scope

**In scope (if obvious):**
- <capability or fix this issue is about>

**Out of scope / explicitly not this issue:**
- <adjacent things that might look related but aren't — helps spec-writer
  hold the line on scope creep>

## Suggested fix direction (non-binding)

<One or more directions worth considering, if any come to mind — spec-writer
and plan-writer own the actual decision; this is a starting point, not a
mandate. Delete if none.>

## References

<File paths, function/skill names, related issues/ADRs/specs, commit
hashes — anything spec-writer would otherwise have to go re-discover.>
